BUSINESS GLOSSARY - Enterprise System
Last Updated: 2024-03-15
Status: Partial (pending review)

==========================================
CUSTOMER MANAGEMENT
==========================================

Customer
An individual or organization that... [definition incomplete]

Client
A person or company that purchases goods or services. (Note: may be used interchangeably with Customer in some contexts)

Account Holder
The person designated as the primary owner of an account. Similar to Customer bu... [TRUNCATED]

triplets.subject: Client
triplets.object: person or company that purchases goods or services

triplets.subject: Account Holder
triplets.object: the primary owner of an account

triplets.subject: Order
triplets.object: request to purchase products or services

triplets.subject: Sales Order
triplets.object: order with confirmed pricing and inventory allocation

triplets.subject: Quote
triplets.object: preliminary order with estimated pricing, not yet confirmed

triplets.subject: SKU
triplets.object: Stock Keeping Unit - a unique identifier for a product variant

triplets.subject: Payment
triplets.predicate: is the transfer of money to settle

triplets.subject: Shipment
triplets.predicate: is the process of delivering goods to

triplets.subject: Delivery
triplets.object: Shipment when goods reach the customer

triplets.subject: Fulfillment
triplets.predicate: is the process of preparing and delivering orders

triplets.subject: CUSTOMERS
triplets.object: customer_id (or is it CustomerID? check with DBA)

entities_post_resolution.name: Order
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Order
A request to purchase products or services. Can be in various states. | Transaction
Similar to Order but more general. May refer to any exchange of value. | Order to Payment: An order may have multiple payment attempts. Or is it one-to-one? | Primary Key: order_id (or OrderID - verify) | | order_id | INTEGER | FK | References ORDERS | | Payment
The transfer of money to settle an order. | Invoice to Order: An invoice is generated for an order. But when? And can one order have multiple invoices? | Fulfillment
The process of preparing and delivering orders. Encompasses Shipment and Delivery. | Customer to Order: A customer can place multiple orders. Or is it accounts that place orders?
